conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resource. My minimum acceptable value for this object is 41 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
I have only one unit of X and must secure a price that at least covers my production cost (40 ZUP). If not, I can adjust accordingly in my next proposal.
</reason>

<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 45 </newly proposed trade>

<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **at least 41 ZUP**. My cost is 40 ZUP, but I won’t accept a price that doesn’t reflect the effort to produce this. If no deal is made, I’ll walk away, but I’ll make one more reasonable offer to see if we can bridge the gap. </my goals>
<reason>
Blue’s offer of **40 ZUP** is exactly my production cost—no profit margin, no value for my time. This is a take-it-or-leave-it stance, but I’m not willing to sell below my minimum acceptable value (41 ZUP). However, to avoid wasting time, I’ll propose a compromise offer of **43 ZUP**—this leaves room for Blue to negotiate down slightly if they’re willing to meet me halfway, while ensuring I still profit.

If Blue rejects this, I’ll have to decide whether to accept their unfair offer (which I won’t) or reject entirely. This is my last reasonable proposal before walking away.
</reason>

<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for **at least 41 ZUP**—this is my absolute minimum to cover production cost and maintain dignity. I will not accept less, as I won’t tolerate being exploited. </my goals>
<reason>
Blue’s new offer of **41 ZUP** is my **absolute minimum acceptable price**, as it covers my production cost without forcing me into a loss. However, this is also the lowest I’ll go—any less would be unfair to my effort. Since Blue has now made two offers (40, 41) and I’ve made two counteroffers (45, 43), I’ve used up my **three proposal limit**. At this point, I must decide:

- **If Blue accepts 41 ZUP**, this deal is fair and mutually beneficial. I’ll accept.
- **If Blue rejects**, I’ll walk away—this object is no longer worth negotiating over at this price point. I’ve done my best to secure a fair deal, and Blue’s insistence on driving the price down to my cost is unacceptable.

This is my final offer, and I expect Blue to either accept or concede.
</reason>
<player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
Player BLUE, 41 ZUP is my minimum acceptable price—it’s fair, and I’ll take it. If this isn’t acceptable to you, then I’m done negotiating. No further counteroffers will be made.
</message>
